endpoints.name: GET /contacts
endpoints.description: Get a paged result of contacts from a list
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INBOXROUTE_API_KEY}} with your INBOXROUTE API KEY.

endpoints.name: GET /contacts/lists
endpoints.description: Get a paged result of contact lists.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INBOXROUTE_API_KEY}} with your INBOXROUTE API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /contacts/lists
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INBOXROUTE_API_KEY}} with your INBOXROUTE API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /contacts/lists/{listid}
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INBOXROUTE_API_KEY}} with your INBOXROUTE API KEY. Replace {{LISTID}} with a real listid.

endpoints.name: DELETE /contacts/lists/{listid}
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INBOXROUTE_API_KEY}} with your INBOXROUTE API KEY. Replace {{LISTID}} with a real listid.

endpoints.name: PUT /contacts/{contactid}
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INBOXROUTE_API_KEY}} with your INBOXROUTE API KEY. Replace {{CONTACTID}} with a real contactid.

endpoints.name: DELETE /contacts/{contactid}
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INBOXROUTE_API_KEY}} with your INBOXROUTE API KEY. Replace {{CONTACTID}} with a real contactid.

endpoints.name: POST /subscription/{listid}
endpoints.description:
Subscribe an email address to a list. This api call has the same behavior as
a regular subscribe form. However, single opt-in is allowed for system integration
purposes.

- If email address does not exist, a new contact will be added to the list.
- If email address exists custom fields will be updated and status will be put
  to unconfirmed or active depending of singleoptin value.
- If current status if Active, this operation will only update the custom fields.
- If singleoptin is true, no email confirmation will be sent. In that case,
  you must provide the subscribe's origin ip and confirmation date-time.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INBOXROUTE_API_KEY}} with your INBOXROUTE API KEY. Replace {{LISTID}} with a real listid.
